name: Real Science Odyssey
description: Secular, hands-on science curriculum with labs, experiments, and short-story-format lessons covering life science, earth science, chemistry, physics, and astronomy.
notes: Level 1 (K-6) and Level 2 (6-10). Parents need to prep experiments but lessons are scripted. Teaches evolution and big bang as fact. No built-in videos or multimedia — pencil-and-paper plus labs. Materials are mostly common household items; optional science kits available.

name: Apologia Science
description: Award-winning Christian science curriculum with conversational textbooks, hands-on experiments, and creation-based perspective for K-12.
notes: Most popular Christian homeschool science curriculum for 11+ years running. Young Explorer series (K-6) is multi-age and Charlotte Mason-style. High school courses are college-prep rigorous. Textbooks are conversational and designed for self-teaching. Video lessons available but expensive ($400-600 extra). Creation-based perspective throughout.

name: Building Foundations of Scientific Understanding (BFSU)
description: Inquiry-based, discussion-driven secular science curriculum in 3 volumes that builds deep scientific understanding through critical thinking and hands-on activities.
notes: Considered the 'gold standard' for secular homeschool science. Three volumes covering K-2, 3-5, and 6-8. Extremely budget-friendly. Requires significant parent preparation and some science background building. Discussion-based and inquiry-driven — not scripted. Volumes must be used sequentially. Exceeds NGSS standards. Parents who are scientists often choose this one.

name: Mystery Science
description: Video-based, inquiry-driven secular science curriculum with guided experiments and discussion prompts for elementary students.
notes: Online streaming video lessons with hands-on activities using common materials. NGSS-aligned. Great for visual/kinesthetic learners and parents without science backgrounds. May only work as complete curriculum through grade 2 — better as supplement for grades 3-5. Lessons range from 5-minute mini-lessons to 45-60 minute full lessons.

name: Noeo Science
description: Literature-based science curriculum combining living books, hands-on experiment kits, and Charlotte Mason methods for grades 1-8.
notes: Complete box includes Instructor's Guide, Student Lab Manual, living books, and experiment kits. 2-day or 4-day schedule options. Christian company but curriculum is worldview-neutral by design — used by both secular and Christian families. No quizzes or tests. Three levels: 1-3, 4-6, 7-8. Covers biology, chemistry, and physics. 60-day trial guarantee.

name: Nancy Larson Science
description: Fully scripted, open-and-go elementary science with hands-on kits, structured lessons, and spiral review — by the author of Saxon Math K-3.
notes: Most scripted science curriculum available — parent reads lesson script aloud while student follows in booklet. Includes complete kit with manipulatives, word wall cards, charts, assessments. Designed 2-3 days/week. Spiral approach revisits topics with increasing depth. Premium price but kids reportedly love it and ask to do science. Best for parents who dread teaching science.

name: Berean Builders
description: Christian creation-based science by Dr. Jay Wile covering K-12 with experiments, multi-level review questions, and science history integration.
notes: By the original Apologia author Dr. Jay Wile. Elementary books cover science through history (5 books, ~3 years each). Three levels of review questions let you use same book across grade ranges. Middle/high school courses are rigorous (Chemistry requires Algebra I). Budget-friendly. Author personally answers student questions via email. Best for grades 3-6+ at elementary level despite K label.

name: Sonlight Science
description: Literature-based Christian science curriculum with living books, Discover & Do experiment kits, and fully planned daily schedules.
notes: Christian company but Science E and some other levels use secular books — parents can share their own viewpoint. Each level spans 3-year age range for multi-age teaching. Discover & Do kits include experiment supplies. 4-day or 5-day schedule options. Presents both creation and evolution perspectives. Reading-heavy — requires parent read-aloud time.

name: Oak Meadow Science
description: Waldorf-inspired secular science woven into a holistic K-12 curriculum emphasizing nature connection, artistic integration, and experiential learning.
notes: Founded 1975 by a trained Waldorf teacher. Science separate from grade 5+; integrated K-4. Nature-focused assignments in early grades. Optional accredited distance learning school with teacher support. 36-week lesson plans with assignment checklists. Expensive for full packages. May feel slow for gifted/advanced learners. Best for creative and kinesthetic learners who thrive with artistic integration.

name: BookShark Science
description: Secular, literature-based science curriculum aligned to NGSS with living books, hands-on activities, and 4-day weekly schedule.
notes: Secular spin-off of Sonlight. NGSS-aligned since 2021 revision. Includes science kit, instructor's guide, and assorted topical books. DVD experiment demos for levels A-E. 36-week, 4-day schedule. Religiously neutral — encourages parent-led worldview discussions. Reusable across siblings (only consumables need replacing). Can be paired with BookShark History for a full secular package.
